title: Lhyfe Becomes First French Player to Obtain European RFNBO Certification - The Renewable Hydrogen Industry's Highest Ecological Standard - For its Production
content:
Lhyfe, a leading producer of green and renewable hydrogen, announced that its Bouin site in VendÃ©e, France (1 MW capacity), has received RFNBO (Renewable Fuel of Non-Biological Origin) certification as of May 23rd. This makes it the first RFNBO-certified hydrogen production site in France. The certification, based on stringent European Commission criteria, confirms that the hydrogen is produced via water electrolysis powered by 100% renewable electricity and that emissions are accounted for across the full lifecycle, including transport.

The first delivery of RFNBO-certified hydrogen took place on May 28, 2025, in Les Sables dâ€™Olonne. This milestone allows Lhyfe's customers to verify the sustainability of the hydrogen they purchase, improve their own carbon footprint, and qualify for national and EU incentive schemes supporting the adoption of clean fuels.

Lhyfe is now pursuing RFNBO certification for its operational French sites in BessiÃ¨res and BulÃ©on, with its German site in SchwÃ¤bisch GmÃ¼nd expected to follow soon. CEO Matthieu GuesnÃ© emphasized the importance of certification for building trust in green hydrogen and enabling transparent decarbonization across Europeâ€™s mobility and industrial sectors.
author: Analysts Account
